frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: MEP amplitude as noisy readout of corticospinal excitability state

core_question: Are individual MEP changes following anodal-tDCS reproducible enough within a single session to serve as a biomarker of tDCS responsiveness?
key_assumptions: MEP amplitude is a valid index of corticospinal excitability in M1
anodal-tDCS exerts a directional effect on CSE that should manifest across sessions
manipulations: two repeated sessions one week apart
key_findings: within-session MEP variance is too large for single-session tDCS-induced CSE change to be a reliable individual biomarker
the across-session direction of CSE change replicates above chance
interpretations: individual tDCS responsiveness requires pooled multi-session MEP measurement to be characterized
limitations_noted: single-session MEP measurement is insufficient for individual-level inference
